page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and specialized compute tasks. The instance achieves a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 4612.53 GB/sec and a large block bandwidth of 200.81 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 31.15 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 314.73 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector performance is supported by a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 66114.92 and an encryption score of 59825.42. For database and web serving workloads, the server handles 69.84 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves 68.5 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

As a member of the c7i-flex family, this server offers a cost-effective alternative to standard compute-optimized instances by utilizing a flexible resource allocation model. While it lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, its high-speed DDR5 memory and AMX instruction support make it highly efficient for CPU-based machine learning tasks, such as running inference on small to medium LLMs, where it achieves 227.81 tokens per second prompt processing on a 7B model. The instance is well-suited for software compilation, database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating at 3.2 GHz, it provides 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show strong performance, with memory read bandwidth reaching 4612.53 GB/sec for small blocks and Redis SET operations performing at 69.84 million operations per second. It also demonstrates capable CPU-based LLM inference, processing 227.81 tokens per second on a 7B model. The c7i-flex.16xlarge is designed as a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads, making it highly suitable for software compilation, database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for databases, web serving, and CPU-based inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A cost-efficient, compute-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four vCPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
Equipped with 128 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
Capable of CPU-based LLM inference, processing 227.81 tokens/sec on 7B models.
